title: Seele no Monshou
wikitext:
[]: Seele no Monshou             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 30, 2005 6:48 am (I don't know if this belongs here or in the discussions area, so if you need to move it. Please do.) We've all seen Seele's coat of arms. The damn thing is plastered on Liliths mask. It's got seven eyes. And has an upside-down isosceles triangle which looks like a humongous nose. Ohhh Yeah. On Liliths mask there'e also a whole load of craters on it as if it had a bad case of acne or something. What's up with that? Anyway. My point is that this is the only version of the mask (along with the stupid multicoloured one) that we are familiar with. However, there is one mask that we only get to see once (maybe twice*). This is the one, as it appeared on screen http://i7.photobucket.com/albums/y283/dirtyhamster/AnnexNo3.jpg I made a bigger version of it myself. Just in case you need a closer look. http://i7.photobucket.com/albums/y283/dirtyhamster/SeelenoMonshou.jpg So what's with this version of the arms, and why go to so much effort to make a new coat of arms if it's only gonna be on screen for a few frames? *Yeah, like I'm gonna check the entire series just for three frames.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon May 30, 2005 8:42 am This is the insignia of the Artificial Evolution Lab (where Yui did the groundwork for Project E), so the "eyes" are replaced by abstract gene/chromosome thingies.

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 30, 2005 8:47 am Shin-seiki wrote: the "eyes" are replaced by abstract gene/chromosome thingies.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon May 30, 2005 8:58 am DirtyHamster wrote: Shin-seiki wrote: the "eyes" are replaced by abstract gene/chromosome thingies.

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 30, 2005 9:12 am Ahhh Jeeees Mannn. I feel like an Idiot now.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Mon May 30, 2005 1:00 pm DirtyHamster wrote: Ahhh Jeeees Mannn. I feel like an Idiot now.

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Tue May 31, 2005 5:17 am Dave wrote: Don't. I never even thought of these questions, let alone the answers! I'm glad someone asked.
